subtitle: Creation, Humanity, and the First Fracture
description: Genesis 1–11 lays the foundation for everything. Watch the Bible Project's animated overview, then sit with what it means — for creation, for the human condition, and for you.

aboutSource.name: The Bible Project
aboutSource.description: The Bible Project creates free animated videos, podcasts, and educational resources that explore the Bible as a unified story leading to Jesus.

days.title: Genesis, Part 1
days.subtitle: In the Beginning

reflection.prompt: From everything you just watched — what moment stayed with you most?

choices.label: The image I was made to carry
choices.deepQuestion: If you truly carry God's image, what would change about how you see yourself — not just today, but in the moments when you feel least seen or most forgotten?

choices.label: How quickly trust broke
choices.deepQuestion: Where in your own life do you recognize that same quiet move — reaching for something that looks good, that bypasses trust in God? What is that costing you right now?

choices.label: That God didn't walk away
choices.deepQuestion: Has there been a season where everything felt broken? What does it mean to you — today — that God moved toward that brokenness instead of away from it?

days.abide: God, you spoke the world into being and called it good. You made us to carry your image — not as a title, but as a calling. And when we broke that, you didn't erase us. You came toward us. Today we want to live like that's true — that we bear your mark, and that grace is already moving.
